children.heading: General authorization
children.label: a General authorization
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out a program, in accordance with this part, to improve the academic quality, institutional management, and fiscal stability of eligible institutions, in order to increase their self-sufficiency and strengthen their capacity to make a substantial contribution to the higher education resources of the Nation.

From the sums available for this part under , the Secretary may award grants to any eligible institution with an application approved under in order to assist such an institution to plan, develop, or implement activities that promise to strengthen the institution.section 1068h(a)(1) of this titlesection 1068 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: which has endowment funds (other than any endowment fund built under as in effect on , and under part B) the market value of which, per full-time equivalent student, is less than the average current market value of the endowment funds, per full-time equivalent student (other than any endowment fund built under as in effect on , and under part B) at similar institutions; orsection 1065 of this titlesection 1065 of this titleSeptember 30, 1986September 30, 1986

children.label: B
children.text: which has expenditures per full-time equivalent student for library materials which is less than the average of the expenditures for library materials per full-time equivalent student by other similarly situated institutions.

children.label: A
children.text: faculty development;

children.label: B
children.text: funds and administrative management;

children.label: C
children.text: development and improvement of academic programs;

children.label: D
children.text: acquisition of equipment for use in strengthening funds management and academic programs;

children.label: E
children.text: joint use of facilities such as libraries and laboratories; and

children.label: F
children.text: student services, including services that will assist in the education of special populations.
Purchase, rental, or lease of scientific or laboratory equipment for educational purposes, including instructional and research purposes.

Construction, maintenance, renovation, and improvement in classrooms, libraries, laboratories, and other instructional facilities, including the integration of computer technology into institutional facilities to create smart buildings.
Support of faculty exchanges, faculty development, and faculty fellowships to assist in attaining advanced degrees in the field of instruction of the faculty.
Development and improvement of academic programs.
Purchase of library books, periodicals, and other educational materials, including telecommunications program material.

Tutoring, counseling, and student service programs designed to improve academic success, including innovative, customized, instruction courses designed to help retain students and move the students rapidly into core courses and through program completion, which may include remedial education and English language instruction.
Education or counseling services designed to improve the financial literacy and economic literacy of students or the students’ families.
Funds management, administrative management, and acquisition of equipment for use in strengthening funds management.
Joint use of facilities, such as laboratories and libraries.
Establishing or improving a development office to strengthen or improve contributions from alumni and the private sector.
Establishing or improving an endowment fund.
Creating or improving facilities for Internet or other distance education technologies, including purchase or rental of telecommunications technology equipment or services.

children.label: A
children.text: contribute to carrying out the purposes of the program assisted under this part; and

children.label: B
children.text: are approved by the Secretary as part of the review and acceptance of such application.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An eligible institution may use not more than 20 percent of the grant funds provided under this part to establish or increase an endowment fund at such institution.

children.heading: Matching requirement
children.label: 2 Matching requirement
children.text: In order to be eligible to use grant funds in accordance with paragraph (1), the eligible institution shall provide matching funds from non-Federal sources, in an amount equal to or greater than the Federal funds used in accordance with paragraph (1), for the establishment or increase of the endowment fund.

children.heading: Comparability
children.label: 3 Comparability
children.text: The provisions of part C, regarding the establishment or increase of an endowment fund, that the Secretary determines are not inconsistent with this subsection, shall apply to funds used under paragraph (1).
